"[I]N TESTING A PLEADING AGAINST A DEMURRER THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE PLEADING ARE DEEMED TO BE TRUE HOWEVER IMPROBABLE THEY MAY BE." (DEL E. WEBB CORP. V. STRUCTURAL MATERIALS CO. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Dimitriou And Associates P.C.
Off Calendar Dec-13-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Helbraun, Esq. 415-982-4000, of Helbraun Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Andrew Dimitriou, Esq. 415-434-1144 of Dimitriou & Associates for Defendant Dimitriou & Associates.
David Dalby, Esq. 415-263-8111 of Hinshaw for Defendants Isaac Jacobson, McLaughlin Yeh LLP.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court shares its views on all four motions. Mr. Dimitriou argues. Mr. Helbraun responds. The Court believes the Plaintiff is entitled to a judgment, and overrules all of the objections of relevance and lack of qualifications to Mr. Bowels's declaration. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF BATROA PROFIT SHARING PLAN (BATROA), MICHAEL LEVINE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Against Dimitriou And Associates P.C.

The Court finds that the bankruptcy filing of Mr. Sullivan does not affect this case other than as to Mr. Sullivan. Motion for Summary Judgment is granted for reasons stated on the record. Parties to meet and confer re: the calculation of pre judgment interest.

Counsel for Plaintiff, Mr. Helbraun to submit a total of 5 different orders for this case:

1. A Proposed order that the bankruptcy filing of Mr. Sullivan does not affect this case other than as to Mr. Sullivan.
2. A Proposed order denying the motion for relief from the August discovery order.
3. A Proposed order denying the motion for leave to file a cross-complaint.
4. A Proposed order denying the motion for consolidation.
5. After meeting and conferring regarding the calculation of statutory Prejudgment Interest, a Proposed order granting the motion for summary judgment.

Defendant may submit a proposed order overruling objections.

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

All Proposed orders to be approved as to form.

Notice Of Motion To Contest And Object To Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar May-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David M. Helbraun, Esq., Helbraun Law Firm, Telephone 415-982-4000, appearing for Plaintiff;
Daria Dub Carlson, Esq., Markun, Zusman, Freniere & Compton, Telephone 310-454-5900, appearing for defendant(s) Jones & Devoy et al; (CourtCall)
Andrew Dimitriou, Esq., Dimitriou & Asscoaites, P.C. Telephone 415-434-1144, appearing on his own behalf as Pro Per for Defendant;

DEFENDANT DIMITRIOU & ASSOC. P.C., ANDREW DIMITRIOU's Motion To Contest And Object To Good Faith Settlement.

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follow

DEFENDANT DIMITRIOU & ASSOC. P.C., ANDREW DIMITRIOU's Motion To Contest And Object To Good Faith Settlement.

Defendants Dimitriou & Associates, PC and Andrew Dimitriou's motion to contest good faith settlement is denied. The settling defendants provide substantial evidence regarding the nature and extent of their liability and Mr. Dimitriou fails to show that the settlement is out of the ballpark. (See Code Civ. Proc. 877.6(d) [the party asserting the lack of good faith shall have the burden of proof on that issue]; Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488, 499; Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1350, n. 6; Toyota Motor Sales USA, Inc. v. Superior Court (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 864, 871;)

The settlors have agreed that they will pay the remaining balance of their policy limits, which they estimate to be approximately $250,000. (Carlson Decl. 3-5.) Further, their declarations show that their other assets are modest. (Jones Decl. 3, 6-13; Jones Decl. (on behalf of Jones & Devoy LLP) 2-6; Cebrian Decl. 4-8.) (See Dole Food Co., Inc. v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 894, 909 [relevant considerations include the financial conditions and insurance policy limits of settling defendants].) In the Contra Costa action that is the basis for this malpractice case, plaintiff sought to recover its $800,000 investment. Even if the Court approximates Plaintiff's approximate total recovery at trial as $1,000,000, the settlement is in the ballpark-that is, within the reasonable range of the settling defendants' proportional share of comparative liability for the plaintiff's injuries. (Hellam v. Crane Co. (2015) 239 Cal.App.4th 851, 864.) While the OSC that caused the dismissal was sent to Mr. Cebrian, it was addressed to him at Mr. Dimitriou's office. (RJN, Ex. 1 at 43, 46.) Mr. Dimitriou caused the confusion in plaintiff's representation by filing a "notice of disassociation," but never truly withdrawing from the representation. (RJN, Ex. 1 at 28; Helbraun Decl., Ex. 5 (Levine's response to special interrogatory 2.) While Mr. Cebrian made special appearances for plaintiffs, he never became the attorney of record and forwarded all notices to Mr. Dimitriou. (Helbraun Decl., Ex. 6 (Cebrian's response to special interrogatory 43.) Further, Mr. Dimitriou was counsel of record on appeal but failed to file a timely opening brief, causing the appeal from the denial of the motion to vacate to be dismissed.

Given this record, the Court finds that Mr. Dimitriou fails to show that the settlement is "out of the ballpark."

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court looks at moving memorandum page 5 line 25, Mr. Dimitriou and Mr. Sullivan's declaration. Plaintiff argues, cites cases.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DIMITRIOU & ASSOC. P.C., ANDREW DIMITRIOU's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT (CCP sec. 430.10)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Andrew Dimitriou and Dimitriou & Assoc. P.C.'s motion to set aside the defaults entered against them on March 21, 2018 is granted and both defaults are now vacated. The sole basis for the opposition to this motion is that the motion is untimely since it was filed more than six months after the defaults were entered. Liberally construing the motion papers, one of the statutory bases for the motion is the mandatory "attorney fault" provision of CCP 473(b) based on the declarations of Andrew Dimitriou and Kevin Sullivan. The six month deadline for obtaining relief per that provision runs from the entry of a default judgment and thus this motion is timely filed since no default judgment has been entered. (Sugasawara v. Newland (1994) 27 Cal. App. 4th 294).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Off Calendar Nov-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Kevin Sullivan'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint (Ccp Sec. 430.10)

Defendant Kevin Sullivan's demurrer to the first amended complaint is OVERRULED. "[I]n testing a pleading against a demurrer the facts alleged in the pleading are deemed to be true however improbable they may be." (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) Yet Sullivan's demurrer would have the Court decide factual disputes in his favor. Even were the facts at issue at the pleading stage, I could take notice of a July 2016 pleading in which Sullivan represented to a court that he was "Attorney for Plaintiffs."

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
